title: Galium cliftonsmithii
taxonomy: Galium cliftonsmithii is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. This family consists of about 600 genera and 13,000 species, and is commonly known as the coffee or madder family. Galium cliftonsmithii is further categorized as a member of the genus Galium, which comprises approximately 400 species of bedstraw and its allies. The genus Galium is found primarily in temperate regions, with a few species extending into tropical and subtropical areas. Its classification places Galium cliftonsmithii among the Rubiaceae family and the genus Galium.

intro: Galium cliftonsmithii, also known as the Yellow Bedstraw, is a perennial herb belonging to the Rubiaceae family, characterized by its yellow flowers and quadrangular stems. This plant has been traditionally used for its health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may aid in wound healing and reducing symptoms of arthritis. The bioactive constituents of Galium cliftonsmithii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and iridoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Galium cliftonsmithii is often prepared as a tincture, infusion, or ointment, which are commonly used to treat various health conditions, including skin irritations and joint pain.
